exercises.heading: Technology
exercises.description: Person A {v} One of the issues I had with this laptop computer was the fact that it didn’t have a CD player. I found this to be a bit absurd as I have a lot of CD’s that I like to play. Call it old school but I think any high-tech electronic should have this type of feature. Other than that, it runs quite smoothly and I love the magnetic connect between the charger cable and the laptop computer itself. {v} Person B {v} No real complaints to be made about this item. My main focus was on its photo features as I wanted to use it to download certain editing software for my photography. It’s easy to connect my camera up to the laptop and get everything working in just a click. The keyboard can be a bit loud but I don’t mind too much. I would think that some customers might want a CD-player, but for me it’s great because the laptop is lighter. {v} Person C {v} I like to blog for fun and so I was looking for something light and travel-friendly and this laptop computer seemed like it was perfect for the job. Since I spend so much time typing for my blog and because I can type really fast, the loud keyboard is actually a problem for me, so I’m considering getting a refund and purchasing something else. But with that aside, the design is great! {v} Person D {v} To be totally honest, working in information technology (IT) means that picking a laptop computer is something I have to take my time with. I spend a lot of my time responding to emails and in chat groups, so buying an attachable mouse was on my list. Although loud, the keyboard doesn’t bother me as I’m always listening to music as I work. I’m satisfied, but next time I’ll be purchasing something more portable.
exercises.subtitle: Four people give their opinions about a new laptop computer that they have purchased. Read their comments and answer the questions below.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks the laptop computer should have a CD-player?
2. Who thinks the laptop is better without a CD-player?
3. Who bought an extra item for their laptop?
4. Who dislikes the loud keyboard?
5. Who thinks that they will get a different laptop?
6. Who bought the laptop for their hobby?
7. Who has nothing negative to say about the laptop computer?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: E. The complexities of moral dilemmas
questions.title: 19. An ethical dilemma is a complicated situation where an individual is required to choose {v} between two options which are both morally correct, but oppose each other. In other {v} words, it’s a decision-making problem where neither of the two options is completely {v} preferable or correct, and the outcome is often uncertain. The complexity of the {v} situation arises from the fact that choosing one option almost always means ignoring the {v} other. Morals and ethics are inseparable, as they are both concerned with the question {v} of right and wrong. Ethical behaviour is more often governed by cultural or societal {v} norms, whereas moral behaviour is determined by an individual’s belief and sense of right {v} and wrong.

answers: A. The history of moral philosophy
questions.title: 20. The study of moral and ethical dilemmas is nothing new. In fact, these questions have {v} been asked since the dawn of time. Ancient Greek writers and philosophers, like Socrates {v} and Plato, date back to nearly 500 B.C., and are still influential today in our modern {v} understanding of moral philosophy. Great thinkers throughout the centuries have {v} struggled with questions of right and wrong, trying to help steer humanity in the correct {v} direction. However, even after centuries of effort, many are still left with questions and {v} uncertainties, frustrated by their inability to truly understand and solve the most {v} significant moral dilemmas.

21. The only way to uncover what a dilemma means is through the identification of the {v} basic dilemmas that an individual suffers from. The five common types of dilemma faced {v} by people in every daily life are classic dilemma, temporal dilemma, orthogonal {v} dilemma, sequential dilemma and unequal dilemma. The toughest test of character that {v} you can face is to have to make decisions when confronted with a dilemma and to be {v} able to live with the consequences. This is because sometimes your decisions can {v} lead to someone being harmed at the cost of saving someone else.

22. In 1842, a ship hit an iceberg and about 30 survivors were crowded into a lifeboat meant {v} to hold seven. As there was a roaring storm, it became quite clear that if anyone were {v} to survive, the lifeboat had to be lightened. The captain reasoned that some of the {v} survivors would have to be thrown off the boat. He said that the decision was not unfair {v} because they would anyway drown if they remained in the boat. The captain decided {v} to sacrifice the weakest individuals. In doing so, he was able to save some of the {v} survivors. However, he was tried for his action.

23. Let’s consider another common dilemma. There’s much debate about the act of looking {v} at their partner’s inbox when left open. Admittedly, it’s something that a lot of people {v} indulge in, but should they? Some would argue that looking at their spouse's inbox is {v} not wrong if one is unsatisfied with the answers or feels that their partner is hiding {v} something. If suspicions are confirmed in the process of reading through these emails, {v} does it really matter that you didn’t have permission in the first place? Clearly these truths {v} would not have been revealed if you hadn’t taken it upon yourself to do some {v} investigating.

answers: H. Thinking from the other’s perspective
questions.title: 24. On the contrary, others might argue there’s an important sacredness to the privacy of {v} one’s emails. A line that ought not be crossed. Since you would disapprove of someone {v} checking your inbox without your permission, you must respect your spouse’s privacy {v} as well. This falls under the well-known “Golden Rule” of treating others as you’d like {v} to be treated. Reading emails without permission breaks trust and may very well lead {v} to major problems in the relationships. An alternative to checking your partner’s emails {v} would be to simply talk to him or her about the things that are bothering you. This {v} encourages open communication and builds trust, rather than destroying it.

answers: F. Ethical dilemmas in a professional setting
questions.title: 25. Another common situation. Company resources are for business purposes and not for {v} your personal use. When working in a company, it is the responsibility of the employee to {v} make sure that the assets and resources of the company are protected and not {v} misused. However, there may be urgent situations when you might require the use of {v} work supplies for your personal reasons. Using work supplies for your own personal {v} purpose is only considered okay when you are not doing it for your own personal gain. {v} Also, you must not make it a habit. It is considered ethically wrong to use company assets {v} and resources for personal use. Special situations may be overlooked.

It makes saving money with coupons and promotions very simple.

questions.title: Question 2: What is his opinion on whether or not people should shop online? {x}
answers: There are too many possible problems that can occur so it is not worth it.
Although there is a bigger variety of products online you can save a lot of time by just going to the store and buying what is in stock there.{v}
There are pros and cons to shopping online so it is important to read reviews of different stores to find what is best for you.
